Off Calendar Oct-25-2021 Continued To Nov-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order For Preliminary Distribution From Blocked Account In 2020 To Allow Expenses To Offset The Gain On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for American Red Cross.
Glen Moss appeared by telephone for Nancy Barfield.

The Court asked Mr. Moss what he wants to do with his petition when the payments they are requesting needed to be made in 2020. Ms. Nelson stated that the main driver of Mr. Moss' petition was to offset capital gains on the sale of the property, that the escape taxes are accruing interest and suggested that the Court grant this part of the petition to stop interest from accruing. Mr. Moss stated that he thinks the Accountant can file a tax return on a fiscal year, that he thinks the petition is still viable, and that he hopes the Court can grant the petition and authorize the Administrator to pay the taxes. Discussion was held regarding the form of the order to withdraw the funds from the blocked account with respect to the payees. Mr. Moss asked that the Court allow Ms. Barfield to be paid. The Court stated that it is reluctant to pay the Administrator when there is so much more to do in the estate. Mr. Moss stated that basically everything has been done, that all of the creditors were paid, that the house was sold and that there is $179,000.00 to distribute. Ms. Nelson stated that she thinks Mr. Moss' request is reasonable especially for payment of the costs Ms. Barfield has incurred. The Court will approve payment for the taxes and payment to the CPA. The Petition for Order for Preliminary Distribution from Blocked Account, etc. is under submission for the Court to consider payment to the Administrator.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order For Preliminary Distribution From Blocked Account In 2020 To Allow Expenses To Offset The Gain On Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Continued To Feb-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Glen Moss, Esq., counsel for Nancy Barfield, Temporary Administrator (appearing by telephone); Stacie Nelson, Esq., counsel for the American Red Cross, beneficiary (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes review of the pleadings and the extraordinary nature of the requested relief in that his client apparently has no authority to act in that her Letters of Administration have expired. Mr. Moss reports on the various events that have occasioned the Petition and the tax consequences that will result from delay in securing additional deductions to offset taxable gains from the sale of the real property before year-end.

Ms. Nelson responds, offering suggestions for proceeding. After further discussion with counsel and the Court Examiner, the Court instructs Mr. Moss to submit new Letters of Administration for filing no later than tomorrow. Mr. Moss must also file an ex parte application for release of the specified funds from blocked account no later than tomorrow. A proposed Order for release of funds must be e-mailed to Ms. Hilton [Court Examiner] no later than Monday, December 28, 2021.

The Court continues the current matter to February 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Order For Preliminary Distribution From Blocked Account In 2020 To Allow Expenses To Offset The Gain On Sale Of Real Property

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Blocked Account
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed, Moving Party Nancy Barfield
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Remery appeared by telphone for Timothy Breen.
Glen Moss appeared by telephone for Nancy Barfield (Special Administrator).
Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for American Red Cross.

Ms. Nelson stated that American Red Cross and the Parties under the petition for intestate succession have reached a settlement in principal, that the estate will be divided in a specific manner, that the holographic Will will be admitted to probate and that they hope to finalize the document real soon. Ms. Nelson also stated that the Parties have a concern, that a capital gains was incurred on the sale of the real property, and that they would like to have the administrative costs and expenses paid in 2020 to offset the capital gains. Mr. Remery stated that the Department of Health Care Services' claim reduced the estate, that it is not economically feasible to do a will contest, and that one third of the estate will go to American Red Cross, one third will go to the intestate Heirs and one third will go to the named Beneficiary. Mr. Remery also stated that they proposed withdrawing funds from the blocked account to pay towards the escape real property taxes as well as the administrative costs and expenses to zero out the $150,000.00 capital gains, that the creditor's claim time has expired and that they have agreed to his Client's petition being denied today and Ms. Barfield being appointed as the general Administrator. The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Timothy Breen on October 29th is denied without prejudice. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The holographic will dated January 30, 2016 is admitted to probate. Nancy Barfield is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with limited authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Moss is to prepare a declaration regarding setting of an earlier hearing and bring the declaration along with the petition to withdraw funds from the blocked account to Room 202 and ask to speak with the assigned Probate Examiner. Ms. Nelson will follow the same procedure so that the petition for approval of the settlement agreement is assigned the same hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Denied

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Blocked Account

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2921 Judah Street, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Glen Moss, Esq., counsel for and with Nancy Barfield, Special Administrator (Mr. Moss appearing by telephone, Ms. Barfield present in Court); David Kurniadi, seller's broker (appearing by video conference); Jonathan Kurniadi, seller's broker (present in Court); Stacie Nelson, for the American Red Cross (appearing by video conference); Kristin Frerichs, Buyer's representative (appearing by video conference); William Remery, Esq. counsel for Tim Green (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes at the outset the purpose of the hearing, to wit: sale of the real property commonly known as 2921 Judah Street, San Francisco, California. Finding that the petition and related pleadings now appear to be in order, which counsel confirm, the Court commences sale of the property, reciting the general terms of the sale and the bid price of $850,000. The Court recites the first overbid at $893,000. After receiving no overbid either in court, over the telephone, or on video conference, the Court confirms the sale at $850,000 and authorizes a 5% commission.

At query by the Court, Mr. Jonathan Kurniadi reports that since this is an all cash offer, he would expect escrow to close in 10 days. The Court court also notes that in lieu of bond, the receipts from the sale will be deposited into a blocked account which Mr. Moss confirms. Mr. Moss reports that the account has not yet been set up but will be on or shortly after September 13, 2020. He also notes that the net proceeds from the sale may be somewhat less than the sale price due to a lien by the State of California in excess of $400,000.
After further discussion, the Court sets hearing for status of the blocked account for September 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. No appearance will be required if the receipt for the account is filed one week before that date.

Mr. Jonathan Kurniadi also reports that the seller's agent made certain cash payments to stop foreclosure sale of the property as well as other efforts and requests a 6% commission instead of the 5% commission. The Court advises Mr. Kurniadi that the request is not properly before the Court at this time but could be pursued by other Court processes.
Mr. Remery advises the Court that the special administrator's temporary letters have expired and should be extended pending hearing on the Petition for Appointment of Administrator on October 19, 2020. Mr. Moss expresses no objection and the Court extends the Temporary Letters of Nancy Barfield to October 19, 2020.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed, Moving Party Nancy Barfield
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for American Red Cross.

The Court noted that there are no other appearances and that the parties seem to want the Court to approve the second amended petition for probate of a lost will but reserve the issue of a subsequent will. Ms. Nelson stated that she does not know why Mr. Moss is not present, that the intestate heirs and the American Red Cross have been in touch, and that they are in the process of getting the matter settled and all agree to the holographic will being admitted. Ms. Nelson also noted that there is petition for intestacy and one for a lost will, stated that the sale of the property is set for Wednesday, and that when the sale goes through, they will be in a better position to settle and will file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement. The Court stated that it does not think it is appropriate for it to act on either petition. Ms. Nelson requested a 30-day continuance. The Petition for Letters of Administration, and the Second Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will are continued to October 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the settlement agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2921 Judah Street, San Francisco, Ca 94122)
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Continued To Sep-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: William Remery appeared by CourtCall for Timothy Breen. Glen Moss appeared by CourtCall for Nancy Barfield who also appeared by CourtCall. Jonathan Kurniadi (Listing Broker) appeared in person. David Kurniadi of The Kurniadi Group (Buyer's Broker) appeared by Zoom. Kristin Frerichs appeared for Catamount Properties 2018, LLC (Buyer). Elizabeth Little appeared by Zoom for the Buyer.

The Court stated that there were two problems with the sale: that the sale was not posted at the courthouse and that special notice to three entities was not given. Mr. Moss stated that the sale was published in the SF Recorder, that he is not sure why there was no proof of publication, and that it was filed. The Court stated that it did not say that it was not published and that there are three special notice entities that weren't provided notice. Mr. Moss stated that the State of California and the Red Cross were given notice. The Court addressed Mr. Moss and stated that he needs to provide proof of notice to the three entities and that there must be posting at the courthouse. The Court then continued the Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (2921 Judah Street, San Francisco, CA 94112) to September 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for posting at the courthouse and proof of notice to the three special notice entities: Accelerated Inheritance, LLC; Stacie P. Nelson; and Probate Advance, LLC. The Court noted that posting at the courthouse would be taken care of by court staff. The Court confirmed for Jonathan Kurniadi that republication was not required. Jonathan Kurniadi noted that the home is in foreclosure and inquired if the Court would advance the hearing. The Court declined noting that it was not optimistic about advancing the hearing. The Court addressed Mr. Moss stating that he would need to secure more than one bank and noted that no single account could be more than $250,000.00. Mr. Moss stated that it is not likely that they would need more than one account because the State of California will receive $400,000.00, that $200,000.00 will go to pay off the mortgage, and that there were also advances from the broker.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasare. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Continued To Jun-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed, Moving Party Nancy Barfield
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Continued To Jun-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Glen Moss for and with Nancy Barfield.
Stacie Nelson appeared by telephone American Red Cross.
William Remery appeared by telephone for Timothy Breen.

The Court noted that the request for appointment of a Probate Referee was supposed to have been submitted, that instead it was filed and that it did not get to the Probate Examiner. The Court also explained the procedure used when appointing a Referee, informed Mr. Moss that it is not appointing Mr. Bornstein, and stated that it does not want Parties to shop for a Probate Referee and that it will not set a precedent for that. In addition, the Court explained the purpose and the requirement of doing publication. Discussion was held regarding the numerous items in Mr. Moss' petition that need to be corrected. Mr. Moss provided courtesy copies of all of the documents that were filed on February 28th and stated that he will file a second amended petition. Mr. Moss also stated that the correct date of death is Sep. 20, 2016. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed are continued to June 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court orders that special letters of administration be issued and extended to June 8, 2020 (no written order shall issue). Ms. Nelson and Mr. Remery stated that they have no opposition to a three-month extension of the special letters to June 8, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-03-2020 Continued To Mar-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
PES-19-303279 The Estate of Sally Christine Owensby.
PES-19-303358 The Estate of Sally Christine Owensby.

Appearances:
William Remery for Petitioner Timothy P. Breen via Court Call.
Glenn Moss for and with Petitioner Nancy Barfield.
Stacie Nelson for American Red Cross.

The Court announced the competing petitions filed in different case numbers. Mr. Moss stated he had problems when he tried to file with both case numbers, that the Trustee sale scheduled for tomorrow will be postponed due to reinstatement. The Court stated Mr. Moss is to submit the proposed order for the matters to be consolidated with PES-19-303279 being the lead case. The Court noted Mr. Moss's proposed order to extend Letters for Special Administration provided in open court does not look like he used the correct format, and will be reviewed after the order for consolidation is filed so all future filings will be in PES-19-303279. Mr. Remery stated he waives approval as to form for the proposed orders for consolidation as well as to extend special administration. Mr. Moss to submit a hard copy of the proposed order with Ms. Nelson's approval as to form to the court on Feb. 04, 2020.

Timothy Breen's Petition for Letters of Adminstration filed in PES-19-303279 and Nancy Barfield's Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed filed in PES-19-303358 are continued to March 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.
